hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card keeps `severity_baseline` and `quality_baseline` on the exact four-level severity dimension system `critical`, `major`, `minor`, `note`, so route-neutral system work cannot smuggle stale XR severity shapes behind matching counts.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `validateSeverityBaseline` checked only the four required XR levels and ignored extra keys, so a synchronized falsification that added `warning: 1` to both `severity_baseline` and `quality_baseline` in a `sysopt.xr-*` backlog card plus its hypothesis-card artifact still verified clean as long as `critical`, `major`, `minor`, and `note` matched `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json`. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any missing or extra XR severity dimensions in both baseline objects, `CLAW/control-plane/system-optimizer/state.json.policy` explicitly requires that exact dimension lock, and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if systems-optimizer verification rejects XR `severity_baseline` or `quality_baseline` objects that add or drop severity dimensions outside the exact `critical`, `major`, `minor`, `note` contract, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR severity baselines are exact four-level vectors, not loose maps; if extra or missing severity dimensions verify clean, route-neutral ratchets can drift away from the authoritative gap dimension system while still claiming baseline alignment.
